description: §41.15.4 (a) compile-time misuse — the `omit:` field-set transform names a field ("passwordHash") that is not present on the User struct (a natural mistake: an author intends to exclude a sensitive column that was never declared on this struct). The type-system stage validates each omit array entry against the struct's field list and rejects the unknown name with E-SCHEMAFOR-OMIT-INVALID-FIELD.
rationale: §41.15.4 normative statement: `omit: ["field", ...]` SHALL accept an array literal of bare field-name strings; the generated table-declaration SHALL include all struct fields except those named; field names not present on the struct SHALL emit E-SCHEMAFOR-OMIT-INVALID-FIELD (§34). Resolution: verify each omit entry matches a declared struct field name.
